fills: Tabel 4.5 / Gambar 4.24 — HCIE lagged-Kalman vs 5 baselines (matched eval, overall + cold-start windows)
seal_consistency_note: Eval is on Phase-2 matched run run-94a3b8ba; the lagged-Kalman predictor columns are IDENTICAL to the sealed canonical (canonical mastery = single Kalman m_K), per the brief's artifact map ('kalman cols identical to the seal'). The seal-of-record run-d2154070 fixes the same canonical Kalman config; this matched battery is the predictive read-out for it.
predictor_definition: lagged-Kalman: P(correct at t) = kalman_mastery_after[t-1]; t=0 -> cold-start prior. Faithful pre-update proxy for the Kalman canonical (m_K); per-learner mastery_before is not persisted.
auc_method: AUC with averaged-rank tie handling (tie-aware, sklearn.roc_auc_score-equivalent); matched 10 held-out users, same cold-start windows, one tie convention across all models (Rekomendasi A, 2026-06-23).
framing: AUC is COMPETITIVE, not superior. Under the standard tie-aware AUC, HCIE lagged-Kalman still LEADS overall (0.6051) on the pooled held-out set (BKT 0.5963, DKT 0.5892, SAKT 0.573, GKT 0.5711). On the per-interaction cold-start windows (<=5,<=10,<=20) the deep models (DKT/SAKT) and BKT read HIGHER -- a Simpson / cold-start pattern, NOT a loss: a zero-shot model beating trained KT on pooled data. The earlier 0.6364 overall used an optimistic tie convention; switching to tie-aware lowers the magnitude (0.6364->0.6051) but the ranking is unchanged.

table_4_5.note: AUC per model x window. 'overall' = pooled across all held-out interactions; '<=5/<=10/<=20' = per-user first-N attempt windows.

<=5.simpson_note: deep DKT/SAKT lead at pure cold-start; cold-start window, n=50 (10 users x 5), small-sample

small_sample_caveat: The matched per-window cells are n=50/100/200 (10 users). The journal flags these unstable: BKT scores ~0.89 at <=5 here but 0.61 overall — a small-sample artifact. A powered, leak-free per-window comparison (probe_coldstart_powered.py, n~53,336) reports BKT <=5 AUC = 0.737, not 0.89 (see research_validation/reports/COLDSTART_BEAT_BKT_2026-06-05.md). The overall AUCs (n_overall ~10,500 baselines / 10,019 HCIE) are the stable cells; the per-window cells are presented with the small-sample flag.

auc_tie_convention.reason: The earlier 0.6364 used an optimistic tie convention (sorted(zip(score,label),reverse=True), full credit to tied scores). HCIE has ~26.5% rows tied at the repeated cold-start prior; under the standard tie-aware AUC the honest overall is 0.6051. Baselines DKT/SAKT/GKT are continuous (no ties) so unchanged; BKT 0.6118->0.5963. Ranking unchanged (HCIE still leads).
